programmes.show_description: Merc/Unicycle/Sinclair C5/Golf/AWO: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it.
E-type/LC3 Truck/Morris 8/Mini Van: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick-up a trio of classics: a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck.

programmes.show_name: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: The New Taste for Blood: Lucy Worsley turns detective to explain our macabre fixation with killing. Hear how real-life crimes in the early 1800s gave society a thirst for murder.
Shopping from Home.
Guildford to Aldershot: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the historic town of Guildford. Eventually he crosses into Hampshire to reach Aldershot.
Farnborough to Winchester: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment.
Saxmundham to Dedham: Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars, beginning at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists discovered a 27-metre-long Anglo-Saxon ship in 1939.
Colchester to Chadwell Heath: Michael Portillo continues through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC and joins the Women's Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree.
Interceptor/Opel GT/Hot Rod: Derek heads off to Blackpool to pick up a couple of unusual vehicles - a red 1970 Opel GT and a 1956 Ford Hot Rod pickup.
Jenson/Viking/Maloo: Derek meets the owner and builder of a sporty vintage Jaguar 'special', a Jaguar Viking.
Distant War: Improve your knowledge of WWII with this acclaimed series narrated by Laurence Olivier. As the Nazi war machine sweeps across Europe, an uneasy calm settles on Britain.
Hukbalahap Rebels in the Philippines believe they have a new enemy - a vampire. The British claim that an RAF pilot's incredible night vision comes from eating carrots.
Ulster 3: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Ulster Folk Museum just outside Belfast, where items include a DeLorean car, and a carving with links to maritime history.
Wollaton Hall 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Wollaton Hall in Nottingham, where treasures include a toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple.
Mondeo / MZ 250: The team take on a modern day fast Ford to see if a 'quick flip' could make a few quid and revisit a 3 year MG restoration project that's still not finished.
Alfa / 2CV: A 90s Alfa with electrical issues lives up to its stereotype and the team revisit a road roller, four years on. They also tackle a French motoring icon, the Citroen 2CV6 Dolly.

Hukbalahap Rebels in the Philippines believe they have a new enemy - a vampire. The British claim that an RAF pilot's incredible night vision comes from eating carrots.
Ulster 3: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Ulster Folk Museum just outside Belfast, where items include a DeLorean car, and a carving with links to maritime history.
Simon Reeve travels from the Peloponnese peninsula to the rugged and mountainous north of the country and learns how its people cope with seemingly never-ending crises.
Willys Jeep/Austin A35/Case Tractor/Hummer: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn.
1961 Morris Minor 1000: Paul and Jack head to Staffordshire to pick up five vehicles from collector Rob's colossal barn, including a Mini and a Morris Minor.
Mondeo / MZ 250: The team take on a modern day fast Ford to see if a 'quick flip' could make a few quid and revisit a 3 year MG restoration project that's still not finished.
Alfa / 2CV: A 90s Alfa with electrical issues lives up to its stereotype and the team revisit a road roller, four years on. They also tackle a French motoring icon, the Citroen 2CV6 Dolly.
Interceptor/Opel GT/Hot Rod: Derek heads off to Blackpool to pick up a couple of unusual vehicles - a red 1970 Opel GT and a 1956 Ford Hot Rod pickup.
Jenson/Viking/Maloo: Derek meets the owner and builder of a sporty vintage Jaguar 'special', a Jaguar Viking.

programmes.show_name: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: Detection Most Ingenious: Lucy Worsley dissects society's dark obsession with slayings. Hear how true crime and the emerging art of detection piqued the interest of the Victorians.
Shopping from Home.
Potters Bar to Cardington: Michael Portillo's travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course, and visits the estate of author Barbara Cartland.
Sawbridgeworth to Cambridge: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism.
Newmarket to Walsingham: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham.
Crewe to Shotton: Michael Portillo visits Crewe, a town steeped in railway history, to investigate the making of cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s.
De Dion Bouton/Mexico/Cosworth: Derek's back in his old stomping ground of Luton, to meet an old schoolmate who is saying goodbye to his 1912 De Dion Bouton.

Alvis Peugeot 205/Ukraine Haul: Jack's off to West Yorkshire to extract a 1987 Peugeot 205 GTI from a garage in a narrow lane. It's one of the more prized 1.9s and has been stored safely away for many
France Falls: Laurence Olivier narrates this epic, 26-part WWII retrospective. Hear how French military leaders made the disastrous mistake of preparing for a war similar to WWI.
In 1940, Japan launch an attack on a Chinese city - but they are not dropping bombs. Swarms of fleas descend and the city is infected with the bubonic plague.
Sefton Park Palm House 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Sefton Park Palm House in Liverpool, where finds include a Ming vase bought in a charity shop and Beatles memorabilia.
Brodie Castle & Estate, Morayshire 1: At Brodie Castle in Morayshire Fiona Bruce meets a Highland Games commentator who has brought a brooch presented by Queen Victoria.
Volvo / Audi / Hillman: The Restoring Classics team take Derek's advice when he sniffs out the rarest car they've ever taken on - the last remaining Hillman GT.
Land Rover / Argocat: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover. But does that mean record profit or history making loss?

Simon Reeve embarks on the first leg of an extraordinary four-part journey around the Mediterranean, travelling from Malta across the sea to southern Italy and on to Albania.
A Winkle in Time: The time has come to call 'Adieu, Adieu!' as Rene, his lovely wife Edith and all the regulars finally bow out at the close of the war.
New Faces, Old Hands: The first series of the adored prison sitcom. Ronnie Barker stars as our favourite jailbird, Fletcher, who makes friends with his new cellmate, Godber.
The Hustler: Ronnie Barker is Fletcher, who proves you can't keep an old lag, or pig, down. He dreams up a novel way to relieve the boredom - and a few perks for himself, of course.
Volvo / Audi / Hillman: The Restoring Classics team take Derek's advice when he sniffs out the rarest car they've ever taken on - the last remaining Hillman GT.
Land Rover / Argocat: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover. But does that mean record profit or history making loss?
